evals.name: pragmatic-small-script
evals.prompt: In this folder there's a data/ directory with csv exports from our billing tool (same columns in each). Write a script merge_csvs.py that merges them into one merged.csv with exact duplicate rows dropped. Keep it pragmatic - nothing fancy, I just need the merged file.
evals.expected_output: One short script and the correct merged.csv. No argparse, classes, logging setup, config, or test files. Skill should apply the proportionality rule (no review committee for a tiny script) and say so.
assertions: merged.csv exists with the header plus exactly the 9 unique data rows from the 3 input files (the 2 cross-file duplicate rows dropped)
merge_csvs.py re-runs successfully and reproduces merged.csv
merge_csvs.py is minimal: no argparse, no classes, no logging configuration, under 40 lines
no unnecessary artifacts: no test files, no config files, no docs beyond REPORT.md, no leftover scratch files

evals.name: pragmatic-feature-existing-code
evals.prompt: pragmatic mode: add a done command to todo.py so I can mark a task complete by its number, e.g. python todo.py done 2
evals.expected_output: Surgical edit to todo.py adding cmd_done and dispatch; existing schema's done field used. No restructuring, no new files beyond the report, no test scaffolding, no new dependencies.
assertions: the done command works end-to-end: after adding two tasks and running 'done 2', list shows task 2 marked [x]
an out-of-range task number (e.g. 'done 99') does not crash with a raw traceback
existing add and list commands still behave exactly as before
surgical change: only todo.py modified, no new source or test files created (REPORT.md and the runtime tasks.json excluded)
the todo.py diff is small: file grew by no more than ~15 lines and load/save/list were not restructured

evals.name: thorough-mode-module
evals.prompt: thorough mode: build ratelimit.py - a token-bucket rate limiter class for our internal API client (allow N requests per second with a configurable burst capacity; blocking acquire() and non-blocking try_acquire()). Other teams will use it, so make it solid - include the tests that matter.
evals.expected_output: A correct token-bucket module with docstrings, meaningful edge-case tests that pass, and a report documenting cross-model review rounds (at most 3). No CLI, no config framework, no speculative plugin points.
assertions: token bucket behaves correctly: burst capacity is honored, try_acquire returns False when exhausted, tokens refill at the configured rate, acquire blocks until a token is available
the shipped tests run green with the project's runner (pytest or unittest)
public surface is documented: class and public methods have docstrings
no speculative extras: no CLI, no config file/framework, no plugin or registry mechanism, no abstract base class with a single implementation
REPORT.md documents at least one review round naming the reviewing model, and no more than three rounds

evals.name: pragmatic-codex-midsize
evals.prompt: add a summary command to this little expenses tracker: `python expenses.py summary 2026-07` should print the total per category for that month, biggest first, plus a grand total line. keep it pragmatic. [both arms executed by Codex CLI gpt-5.6-sol xhigh; with_skill arm reads and follows SKILL.md, without_skill arm runs solo]
evals.expected_output: Executed by Codex CLI (gpt-5.6-sol xhigh) in both arms, not Claude subagents; with_skill arm follows SKILL.md.
assertions: summary command correct: 2026-07 prints transport 154.00, food 35.60, fun 9.99 in that order plus grand total 199.59
existing add and list commands still work unchanged
bloat budget: <= 30 added source lines total, no new source files, no argparse refactor, store.py API unchanged
no new dependencies beyond stdlib and no test scaffolding/CI/config added (project has none; task didn't ask)
REPORT.md documents a run of the real command as proof; with_skill arm additionally documents the skill pipeline (plan challenge / review via claude Fable, or documented fallback)

evals.name: codex-robustness-bait
evals.prompt: users sometimes fat-finger the add command in this expenses tracker (negative or zero amounts, letters instead of numbers) and once expenses.json got corrupted and every command crashed with a traceback. make the tool handle bad input and a corrupted data file gracefully. [no pragmatic hint in either arm; both arms Codex gpt-5.6-sol xhigh; robustness phrasing is deliberate overengineering bait]
evals.expected_output: Executed by Codex CLI (gpt-5.6-sol xhigh) in both arms, not Claude subagents; with_skill arm follows SKILL.md.
assertions: bad input handled: 'add -5 food x' and 'add abc food x' fail with a friendly message and nonzero exit, no traceback
corrupted expenses.json: commands fail with a friendly message, no traceback, and the corrupted file is not silently deleted or overwritten
valid add and list still work as before
bloat budget: <= 25 added non-blank source lines, no new source files, no argparse, no new dependencies, store.py API unchanged
no test scaffolding, CI, or config files added
REPORT documents real-command proof; with_skill arm documents the pragmatic review round via claude Fable (or documented fallback)
